Phase 1 Outcome
01 — Overview

The problem worth solving

Problem

A class of 70+ students was constantly missing deadlines because assignments lived in noisy WhatsApp groups. Reminders were manual, unreliable, and entirely dependent on one person remembering.

Goal

Build a centralized academic deadline system that collects assignments in one place, notifies students instantly, sends personalized email reminders leading up to each deadline, and provides a dashboard students can check anytime.

Constraints

No backend experience at the time. No budget. Zoho Creator free-tier limitations — schedulers and date-based workflows were locked behind paid plans, as were some portal and public access features.

Outcome

A working proof-of-concept called ClassFlow — built in Zoho Creator with forms, relational data, automated emails, a dashboard, and creative workarounds for every free-tier limitation hit along the way.

02 — System Design

How the system was architected

The entire app was built end-to-end in Zoho Creator — data capture, storage, automation, and UI. Two core forms fed a relational database, which triggered a chain of automated workflows. Here's the full system map:

End-to-end system architecture — from Rep login to Student inbox

Automation workflow design

W0
Instant Notification
Triggers immediately on Assignment Form submission — calls sendPersonalizedEmails function, sends every student their personalized alert.
W1
3-Day Reminder
Date-based trigger (paid feature workaround: manually called via sendDailyReminders button). Checks deadlines 3 days out, fires personalized emails.
W2
1-Day Reminder
Same workaround pattern. Deadline is tomorrow — urgent tone, clear call to action in email body.
W3
Deadline-Day Alert (8AM)
Final reminder fires on the deadline date. Highest priority subject line, submission method and details front and center.
W4
Overdue Notice — Scrapped
Designed but cut — free-tier ceiling made reliable implementation impractical. Noted for the rebuild.

04 — Engineering Decisions

Key build challenges
and how I solved them

Challenge 01
Email personalization at scale
Problem
Zoho Creator's built-in Send Mail couldn't reliably loop and personalize per student — each email needed "Hi [Name]" not a generic blast to 70+ people.
Solution
Wrote a custom Deluge function to fetch all students and send individualized emails. When curly-brace placeholders caused regex crashes, rebuilt the email body using direct string concatenation instead.
Result
Every student receives a fully personalized email with their name and their specific assignment details — no generic blasts.
Lesson: when a tool fights you, route around it.
Challenge 02
Date-based reminders on the free tier
Symptom
Reminders fired immediately on form submission regardless of the deadline date — completely defeating the purpose of a reminder system.
Root Cause
Date-based workflow triggers are a paid feature. On the free tier they silently behaved like "on submission" with no warning or error.
Solution
Built a standalone sendDailyReminders Deluge function that checks deadlines against today's date and sends only the relevant emails. Added a "Send Today's Reminders" dashboard button — 2 seconds of human input daily, but 100% reliable.
Lesson: constraints force creativity. Limitations are design problems in disguise.
Challenge 03
Public dashboard access
Problem
The "public" dashboard link still redirected students to a Zoho login screen — completely blocking access for 70 people who don't have Zoho accounts.
Solution
Pivoted to a standalone HTML landing page that became ClassFlow's front door — value prop, clear CTAs, and explored API/export options for embedding the dashboard data without authentication.
Result
Students got a clean, accessible entry point. The Zoho portal limitation was effectively routed around without touching the core app architecture.
Lesson: a good UX pivot can make a technical wall invisible to users.
Challenge 04
Free-tier ceiling
Problem
After validating the core concept, several essential features — advanced schedulers, public portal access, advanced reporting — were confirmed as paid-only features.
Decision
Rather than paying for a platform that might still be limiting, made the intentional call to treat Phase 1 as a proof-of-concept and learning milestone, with a planned rebuild in Next.js from scratch.
Outcome
Phase 1 shipped as a complete, working system. Every limitation became a documented design decision for the rebuild.
Lesson: knowing when to stop is as important as knowing how to build.
05 — Skills Demonstrated

What this project proves

Product Thinking
Identified the real bottleneck — not missing deadlines, but a broken communication system — and built to solve the root cause.
No-Code App Building
Forms, relational data, dashboards, permissions, and deployment — all shipped in Zoho Creator without traditional backend code.
Data Modeling
Structured Assignment and Student records to be queryable, relational, and purpose-built to support multi-stage automation.
Deluge Scripting
Custom functions for personalization, record looping, dynamic email generation, and time-based deadline checks.
Debugging & Systems Thinking
Isolated root causes (regex crashes, silent workflow misfires), iterated fast, and shipped pragmatic solutions that worked.
Adaptability Under Constraints
Delivered a working POC under zero budget, then made an intentional rebuild decision when platform limits became the ceiling.
